raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
November 2024 actuals came in at $627,900 against a budget of $550,000, a $77,900 (+14.2%) unfavourable variance. The overrun is broad-based rather than driven by a single event: five salary lines and one contractor line all exceeded budget, with each having a distinct and documentable driver.
The salary overage of $49,400 across Engineering, Product, Marketing, Operations, and G&A reflects a mix of early onboarding (Engineering), a transition double-count (Product), a misclassified bonus payment (Marketing), approved seasonal staffing (Operations), and a year-end contractor posting to salaries (G&A). Most of these are either one-month timing items or already-approved decisions, though the Marketing bonus posting warrants a coding correction.
Contractor Spend exceeded budget by $18,500 (+92.5%), driven entirely by the approved Platform Migration project (PLAT-001): two contracted specialists engaged from late October plus a formally approved amendment for an AWS migration specialist. All spend sits within the project's $50,000 total approved budget and the engagement is on track for a January go-live.
No evidence of unplanned or unapproved spend was found. However, four salary lines (Product, Operations, G&A, and a misclassification in Marketing) carry forward implications into December that Finance may wish to confirm against the current headcount plan.

LINE COMMENTARY

Engineering Salaries | +$18,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $120,000 | Actual: $138,000
The $18,000 overrun is driven by the onboarding of Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) at a $185,000 base salary. Her start date was brought forward to 28 October at her request, ahead of the planned 4 November start, meaning her first full payroll run landed in November. The budget had been set assuming a November start with a partial-month cost; the early start created a full-month salary charge instead. This is a one-off timing item with no ongoing budget impact from December onwards, as the budget will have been set to reflect her full-month run rate.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z - gmail-engineering_salaries-broad - Offer letter to Priya Sharma — start date 28 October per her request. Original plan was 4 November. First full payroll: November.
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z - slack-engineering_salaries-broad - Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) started 28 October — first full salary run hits November. Comp: $185K base. Previous plan assumed November start but she onboarded a week early, creating one extra pay period overlap.

Product Salaries | +$12,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $92,000
The $12,000 overrun reflects a one-month double-salary event: outgoing PM Marcus served his notice through 30 November while incoming PM Sarah started on 1 November, resulting in both salaries being on payroll simultaneously for the full month. Marcus is expected to have exited at month-end, so the double-count is likely limited to November only. No formal email evidence was found; this is sourced from HR Slack. Finance should confirm Marcus's final payroll date to validate that December returns to the single-headcount budget.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-product_salaries-broad - Note on Product: Marcus (outgoing PM) is serving his notice through 30 November. Sarah (incoming PM) started 1 November. Double salary in November only — Marcus exits end of month.

Marketing Salaries | +$8,400 (+14.0%) | Budget: $60,000 | Actual: $68,400
The entire $8,400 variance is attributable to Q4 performance bonuses paid on 15 November to the Marketing team, based on approved Q3 performance review outcomes. The Slack evidence notes these were posted to the Marketing Salaries line rather than a bonus or variable compensation line. This is a likely misclassification: the underlying spend is approved, but the coding may distort both the salaries and bonus budget lines. Finance should consider a reclassification journal to the appropriate bonus line. The base salary run rate appears to be on budget.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z - slack-marketing_salaries-broad - Q4 performance bonuses paid 15 November for Marketing team. $8,400 total across the team per the approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Separate from base salary line — should have been budgeted under bonuses but posted to salaries.

Operations Salaries | +$7,000 (+17.5%) | Budget: $40,000 | Actual: $47,000
The $7,000 overrun reflects five temporary warehouse staff engaged through an agency for peak season (November and December), with costs posting to Operations Salaries per the note in the ops channel. The Slack message states this was planned in an operations budget review but was not reflected in the original headcount budget. The variance is therefore expected to repeat in December at a similar level. Finance should confirm whether the operations budget review that approved this spend resulted in a formal budget amendment or whether December will carry the same unfavourable variance.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-operations_salaries-broad - Peak season starts — 5 temporary warehouse staff added for November and December. Bringing in via agency, posting to Ops Salaries. Cost: $7,000 additional per month. Planned in operations budget review, not reflected in original headcount plan.

G&A Salaries | +$4,000 (+13.3%) | Budget: $30,000 | Actual: $34,000
The $4,000 overrun corresponds exactly to the monthly cost of Clara Mendez, a finance contractor brought in for Q4 year-end close support on a three-month engagement at $4,000 per month. The CFO directed this spend to post to G&A Salaries. The engagement is expected to run through at least January, meaning this line is likely to carry a similar variance in December and Q1. No formal email approval was found; sourced from the finance Slack channel. Finance may wish to confirm whether this posting treatment is intentional or whether a reclassification to Contractor Spend would better reflect the nature of the cost.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-12T12:15:00Z - slack-g&a_salaries-broad - Brought in Clara Mendez (finance contractor) for Q4 close support. $4,000/month, posting to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. Year-end close support, 3-month engagement.

Contractor Spend | +$18,500 (+92.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $38,500
The $18,500 overrun is fully attributable to the Platform Migration project (PLAT-001), a CTO- and CFO-approved engagement. The spend comprises two elements: (1) the original SOW with TechForce Ltd (DevOps) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend) at day rates of $750 and $700 respectively, engaged for an eight-week period from 28 October; and (2) a formally approved SOW Amendment 1 for an AWS migration specialist engaged for two additional weeks at $6,500, approved by the CTO on 15 November. The CRM records $18,500 posted against PLAT-001's $50,000 total approved project budget, with Milestone 2 of 4 complete and a January go-live currently on track. The monthly budget of $20,000 appears to have been set before the project scope was finalised; remaining project spend may continue to exceed the monthly line against this budget. Finance should track remaining PLAT-001 spend against the $50,000 project envelope rather than the monthly contractor budget line to avoid repeated unfavourable variances through go-live.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:15:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-broad - SOW executed with TechForce Ltd (DevOps contractor) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend contractor). 8-week engagement from 28 Oct. Day rate schedule attached. CTO and CFO co-signed.
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-contractor_spend-broad - Platform migration kicked off — two senior contractors (DevOps + Backend) engaged for 8 weeks from 28 Oct. Day rates $750 and $700 per day respectively. Estimated Nov cost ~$12,000. CTO approved.
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:45:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-narrow - CTO email — AWS migration specialist engaged for 2 additional weeks. $6,500 total. Approved. Added to existing SOW as Amendment 1.
- CRM - 2024-11-16T26:15:00Z - crm-contractor_spend-broad - Platform Migration Project (PLAT-001). Status: In Progress. Contractor spend Nov: $18,500 of $50K approved budget. Milestone 2 of 4 complete. On track for January go-live.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are within acceptable tolerance and require no further commentary at this time:
- **Sales Salaries** — $6,000 (+4.0%) favourable versus budget. Within normal run-rate range.
- **Benefits & Payroll Taxes** — $4,000 (+8.0%) unfavourable. Likely correlated with the salary overruns noted above; no independent review required at this stage.
